methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Shinkei is a Series A consumer technology company founded by Saif Khawaja and Reed Ginsberg that has raised $30M to date. The comp universe consists of two private frontier-tech peers—Daylight Computer and Whisper Aero—with no direct public comparables available in the consumer tech sector at this early stage.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $30M as a proxy for current value, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $60M. This method reflects the expected value creation trajectory between funding rounds without reliance on earnings or revenue multiples unavailable for early-stage consumer tech firms.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M to $69M, with a median of $60M. The range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression result, accounting for execution risk and market conditions in frontier consumer tech. Key uncertainties include limited comparable data (two private peers only, zero public comps), reliance on a single applicable method, and absence of revenue or unit-economics data. A Series B fundraise would provide superior clarity through market-tested pricing.
